narrative.headline: What 2014 Chevrolet Traverse owners report most
narrative.summary: Air bag warning lights and related gauge malfunctions are the most commonly reported concern among owners of this vehicle, often appearing alongside fuel gauge irregularities. Electrical system faults — primarily the infotainment screen going blank and losing Bluetooth connectivity — make up the next most frequent pattern. Engine stalls and check-engine warnings, as well as stability control and traction-control alerts accompanied by vibration and unintended braking, round out the primary complaint picture.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: Owners report the air bag sensor warning light illuminating intermittently, sometimes alongside fuel gauge and other dashboard gauge malfunctions. · 5 crash · 12 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The radio or infotainment display goes to a blank screen, causing loss of Bluetooth functions, with dealers reportedly unable to resolve the problem. · 1 crash

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners describe the engine stalling while stopped, accompanied by the engine warning light illuminating, with dealers finding no definitive cause during diagnostic testing. · 1 fire · 1 crash

issues.title: Electronic Stability Control (Esc)
issues.blurb: Owners report sudden vibration during acceleration followed by stability control and traction error messages, check engine light activation, a burning smell, and what appears to be random unintended braking triggered by the control system.
